draft:
Last week I ran the Sovereign Leadership workshop with NLDigital in Amsterdam — a room of senior leaders working through what AI governance actually means when your name is on the decisions.

**AI leadership is a sovereignty practice, not a technology decision.** The leaders who will still be in charge in 2030 are the ones who decide what AI is for in their specific context — not the ones who follow vendor roadmaps. The 6-pillar CoE framework is the tool for making those decisions visible, deliberate, and defensible. The question I kept returning to: which AI decisions in your organization does a human actually own — not approve, but own? Most rooms go quiet at that point. This one didn't.

Every attendee left with a Personal AI Sovereignty Assessment — their own decisions mapped, delegation choices made explicit, and a Q3 commitment written in.

[Run this with your organization → frankx.ai/workshops/sovereign-leadership-nldigital-2026-05-19 | Book a session → frankx.ai/workshops/sovereign-leadership#book]
